title: passiflora foetida var. hirsuta
intro: Passiflora foetida var. hirsuta is a variety of the Passiflora foetida plant, commonly known as the "stinking passionflower" due to its strong, unpleasant odor. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Passiflora foetida var. hirsuta include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to aid in digestion and reduce fever. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or dried powder, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their effects.
